//: gnoblin session mode. The low-patch way to strip GNOME Shell's own UI.
//parentMode: Inherit everything from the stock 'user' mode (WM, keybinds,
//parentMode2: workspaces, windows, notifications, unlock dialog), then override.
//panel: Empty the top bar. No activities button, no clock, no quick settings,
//panel2: no system indicators. Quickshell (kobel) draws the real bar as a
//panel3: layer-shell surface. The leftover empty panel actor is hidden by the
//hasOverview: No Activities overview, dash, app-grid, hot corner, or overlay key.
//hasOverview2: Inherited false from 'user', restated here so the strip is explicit
//hasOverview3: and survives even if the parent mode changes upstream.
//showWelcomeDialog2: gates it on sessionMode.showWelcomeDialog, true in the 'user' parent).
//showWelcomeDialog3: gnoblin owns onboarding via its own chrome; the stock modal has no place.
//components: Keep only the essential background agents so polkit prompts,
//components2: the keyring, auto-mount and NetworkManager still work. Everything
//components3: visual (bar/dash/overview) is gone; these are headless services.
//components4: gnoblinControl hosts the org.gnoblin.* control protocol — first-class
//components5: gnoblin source (src/gnome-shell-overlay), loaded like any core component.
